symptom: Customer deleted a Storage Account and needs recovery
rootCause: Storage Account was deleted (individually, via RG deletion, bulk delete, or deployment in complete mode)
solution: Storage Account is self-recoverable within 14 days of deletion. Customer must first attempt self-recovery via Azure Portal (CSAR): https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/storage/common/storage-account-recover. If self-recovery fails due to Azure Policy enforcing restricted network access, assist with reviewing/exempting the policy assignment. If other issues persist, create collaboration with Storage CSS (SAP: Azure/Storage Account Management/Deletion and Recovery/Recover deleted storage account).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureDev/Dev_ARM:/[ARM] Azure Resource Manager (ARM)/Support Topics/Resource Management/Recovering deleted Resource Group
sourceUrl: https://dev.azure.com/Supportability/AzureDev/_wiki/wikis/Dev_ARM?pagePath=/[ARM] Azure Resource Manager (ARM)/Support Topics/Resource Management/Recovering deleted Resource Group

symptom: Customer deleted Network resources (Microsoft.Network/*) and needs recovery
rootCause: Network resources (Public IPs, VNets, NSGs, etc.) were deleted
solution: Most Microsoft.Network resources are NON-RECOVERABLE. Public IPs cannot be recovered. Other NRP resources can be recreated (not recovered) by the customer. If customer asks for JSON properties of deleted resources, set best-effort expectation and follow NRP TSG to retrieve data from Kusto (may need specific cluster permissions). For DNS zones, properties cannot be obtained via standard process — collaboration with Network team is needed. NRP TSG: https://supportability.visualstudio.com/AzureNetworking/_wiki/wikis/Wiki/597739/How-to-recover-deleted-resources-
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureDev/Dev_ARM:/[ARM] Azure Resource Manager (ARM)/Support Topics/Resource Management/Recovering deleted Resource Group

If customer has further questions or issues with soft-delete recovery, create collaboration with Key Vault CSS. TSG: https://supportability.visualstudio.com/AzureAD/_wiki/wikis/AzureAD/1285293/Azure-Key-Vault-Soft-Delete-Troubleshooting-Workflow

symptom: Azure Stack Hub secret rotation fails with 403 Forbidden StorageException ('Server failed to authenticate the request') and ARM/Portal becomes inaccessible — Test-AzureStack AzsStorageDataPlane shows failures
rootCause: When storage external/internal certs are expired or storage service is unhealthy, WAS role attempts to call SRP and grab 'key1' while secret rotation expects 'key2'. This causes storage context creation to fail with AuthenticationFailed error, impacting ARM and Portal functionality.
solution: Run RepairConfigure on WASP/WASPUBLIC roles via unlocked PEP session: 1) Create custom action plan XML with WAS Configure (Step 0) and WASPUBLIC Configure (Step 1) RepairConfigure tasks. 2) Save to C:\RepairConfigure.xml. 3) Execute via Invoke-ActionPlanInstance -ActionPlanPath $actionPlanPath -TimeoutInSecs 60 -WaitForResult. 4) Verify with Test-AzureStack -include AzsStorageDataPlane. Portal should become accessible after.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Stack Hub/Known Issues/151614 - Secret rotation failed due to ARM Portal issue
sourceUrl: https://dev.azure.com/Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/_wiki/wikis/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki?pagePath=/Azure Stack Hub/Known Issues/151614 - Secret rotation failed due to ARM Portal issue

symptom: Need to verify customer provided the correct ARM template for a deployment — how to calculate and compare template hash
rootCause: Every ARM deployment generates a hash (Murmurhash64) based on the template content. The hash is stored in ARMPRODGBL Kusto cluster > Deployments database > Deployments table > templateHash property.
solution: Use the REST API 'Deployments - Calculate Template Hash' (https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/rest/api/resources/deployments/calculate-template-hash) to calculate the hash of a template without deploying. Compare the calculated hash against the templateHash in Kusto (ARMPRODGBL > Deployments > Deployments table) to verify the customer provided the correct template.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureDev/Dev_ARM:/[ARM] Azure Resource Manager (ARM)/Troubleshoting Guides/Get ARM template hash
sourceUrl: https://dev.azure.com/Supportability/AzureDev/_wiki/wikis/Dev_ARM?pagePath=/[ARM] Azure Resource Manager (ARM)/Troubleshoting Guides/Get ARM template hash

symptom: Tags fail to save when using Azure Portal or PATCH operation on certain resource types (e.g. AlertRules, PostgreSQL, OMS, IP Groups, Azure Frontdoor, DataMigration, Insights Workbooks). Portal always uses PATCH, so tagging fails through portal UI.
rootCause: Some resource providers do not implement the PATCH operation for tags. ARM passes the tag operation to the RP, and if the RP doesn't support PATCH, it fails.
solution: Use PUT operation instead via PowerShell/CLI/REST API. Example: $Resource = Get-AzResource -ResourceGroupName testrg; $Resource | ForEach-Object { $_.Tags.Add('testkey','testval') }; $Resource | Set-AzResource -Force
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureDev/Dev_ARM:/[ARM] Azure Resource Manager (ARM)/Support Topics/Tags

symptom: Setting tags on AlertRules (microsoft.insights/alertrules) fails with error: UnsupportedRequestContent - Request content is not well formed or supported
rootCause: AlertRules RP returns an invalid 'action' property on GET that causes subsequent PUT to fail. Also 'metricNamespace' in dataSource may cause issues.
solution: Remove invalid properties before PUT: $r = Get-AzResource -ResourceId {id}; $r.Properties.PSObject.Properties.remove('action'); $r.Properties.condition.dataSource.PSObject.Properties.remove('metricNamespace'); $r | Set-AzResource -Tag @{'key'='value'} -Debug
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureDev/Dev_ARM:/[ARM] Azure Resource Manager (ARM)/Support Topics/Tags

symptom: Setting tags on App Service Certificates (Microsoft.Web/certificates) via PowerShell fails with error: MissingApiVersionParameter - The api-version query parameter is required for all requests
rootCause: PowerShell bug with URL encoding of '#' character in resource IDs. The '#' breaks the URL parsing.
solution: Replace '#' with '%23' in the resource ID before calling Set-AzResource: $resource.ResourceId = $resource.ResourceId.Replace('#','%23'); then call Set-AzResource with the modified ResourceId
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureDev/Dev_ARM:/[ARM] Azure Resource Manager (ARM)/Support Topics/Tags

symptom: Tag names with underscore characters get modified from uppercase to lowercase (e.g. 'ROLE_PURPOSE' becomes 'rolE_PURPOSE') on Portal dashboards (Microsoft.Portal/dashboards) and metric alerts (microsoft.insights/metricAlerts)
rootCause: Resource provider bug that modifies tag name casing when underscore characters are present
solution: No fix available - known RP-side bug. Workaround: avoid underscore characters in tag names or accept the casing change.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureDev/Dev_ARM:/[ARM] Azure Resource Manager (ARM)/Support Topics/Tags

symptom: Tags with spaces in the name are silently discarded on Traffic Manager (Microsoft.Network/trafficManagerProfiles). PATCH returns HTTP 200 success but the tag is not actually stored.
rootCause: Traffic Manager resource provider silently ignores tags that have spaces in the tag name
solution: Do not use spaces in tag names for Traffic Manager resources. File ICM against NRP to fix the silent discard behavior.

rootCause: Network Watcher is a hidden resource type that doesn't expose tag editing from its own resource blade
solution: Go to All Resources in portal → click 'Show hidden types' checkbox → find the Network Watcher resource → use the 'Assign tags' button at the top

symptom: Kubernetes master and worker nodes on Azure Stack Hub fail to communicate with ARM endpoint during deployment. K8s may fail with connectivity exceptions to management endpoint.
rootCause: Management ARM endpoint IP falls within the CIDR range of the virtual network/subnet that Kubernetes is deployed against, causing nodes to route traffic internally instead of to the public management ARM IP. Default AKS creates 10.240.0.0/16 subnet which may overlap with management endpoint.
solution: Use a custom virtual network with AKS engine and ensure the management ARM endpoint IP does not fall within the CIDR of the custom virtual network and subnets. Reference: https://github.com/Azure/aks-engine-azurestack/blob/master/docs/tutorials/custom-vnet.md
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Stack Hub/Known Issues/151505 - Kubernetes can not reach the arm endpoint from master nodes

symptom: ARM cache is out of sync with resource provider - resource exists in RP but not visible in ARM, or resource deleted from RP but still appears in ARM
rootCause: ARM maintains a cache of resource state that can become desynchronized from the actual resource provider state
solution: Use Jarvis 'Synchronize resource state' action at resource scope. Use provisioning state 'Create' when resource exists in RP but not ARM, or 'Delete' when resource exists in ARM but not RP. Action URL: https://portal.microsoftgeneva.com/EEA553D5
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureDev/Dev_ARM:/[ARM] Azure Resource Manager (ARM)/Troubleshoting Guides/ARM cache synchronization

symptom: API calls to ARM endpoint return HTTP 429 (Too Many Requests). The failureCause field in HttpIncomingRequests is 'gateway', and 429 is NOT present in HttpOutgoingRequests.
rootCause: Client exceeded ARM request limits (reads: 12000/hour, writes: 1200/hour per principal per subscription). ARM throttling is enforced at the gateway level. Note: some RPs override ARM limits via throttlingRules in their manifest (e.g., Microsoft.Insights x-ms-ratelimit-remaining-subscription-resource-requests header).
solution: ARM API call limits are hard limits and cannot be adjusted. Solutions: (1) Optimize call patterns - e.g., replace polling VMs every 2 seconds with Azure Monitor alerts. (2) Split calls across multiple service principals. (3) Review x-ms-ratelimit-remaining-* response headers to track remaining quota.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureDev/Dev_ARM:/[ARM] Azure Resource Manager (ARM)/Support Topics/Throttling/Throttling (Error 429) response from ARM

symptom: API calls return HTTP 429 (Too Many Requests). Error 429 is present in HttpOutgoingRequests table, matching the customer's throttled requests.
rootCause: Resource Provider (RP) is throttling the requests at the RP layer, not ARM. The 429 responses originate from the RP endpoint visible in the hostName column of HttpOutgoingRequests.
solution: Transfer the case to the owning RP team. RP-side throttle limits can sometimes be adjusted. Use Kusto query on HttpOutgoingRequests filtered by subscriptionId + httpStatusCode==429, summarize by hostName to identify which RP is throttling.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureDev/Dev_ARM:/[ARM] Azure Resource Manager (ARM)/Support Topics/Throttling/Throttling (Error 429) response from ARM

symptom: Azure Stack Hub internal secret rotation fails at step 'Rotate Secret for Azure Stack ARM and Portal Services' with error: 'Timeout of 7200 seconds reached for interface RotateSecret'. Exception is System.TimeoutException in WAS RotateSecret task.
rootCause: Some resource provider endpoints (e.g., MySQLAdapter, App Service, SQL RP) are not reachable from WAS VMs, blocking secret rotation. Common causes: XRP VMs are off or unhealthy, RP VMs are off, RP certificates expired, or orphaned RP resources.
solution: (1) Collect Wait-AcceptResourceManagerClientCertificate log from WAS01 to identify unreachable RP endpoints. (2) Use Test-NetConnection from WAS01/WAS02 to confirm endpoint unreachability. (3) Fix the underlying RP issue (restart VMs, renew certs, clean orphan resources). (4) Re-run internal secret rotation after confirming endpoints are reachable.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Stack Hub/Components/Identity and Security/Internal Secret rotation fails at Rotate Secret for Azure Stack ARM and Portal Services

symptom: Azure Stack Hub VMs that were previously resized (without deallocate) on builds 2301-2406 experience unexpected reboots when performing minor ARM operations (attach/detach disk, modify NSG, add tags). Temp disk inside VM shows incorrect size (e.g., 50GB instead of expected 100GB for Standard_D2).
rootCause: Code path issue between CRP and storage infrastructure starting in build 2301 caused temp disk resize to fail silently. The storage controller did not update the temp disk size correctly, creating metadata inconsistency. CRP validation detects this mismatch during subsequent ARM operations and triggers a VM restart.
solution: (1) Upgrade Azure Stack Hub to build 2408 or later (contains fix PR 10194138). (2) After upgrade, reboot each affected 'dirty' VM (Restart-AzVM, deallocate not required) to correct temp disk size. (3) Verify temp disk size matches expected value after reboot. Pre-2408 workaround: deallocate VM then start again to force temp disk recreation.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Stack Hub/Known Issues/159042 - Previously resized VMs may restart unexpectedly during other ARM operations

symptom: Azure Policy using `not { notEquals }` pattern on array aliases (e.g. `ipRules[*].address`) returns false positives — condition evaluates as true even though no single array element satisfies all required conditions simultaneously
rootCause: Array aliases evaluate all elements simultaneously. `not { notEquals }` checks if at least one element equals the value. When two independent conditions are checked across different array positions, they can each match different elements and both return true, producing a false positive that looks like one element satisfies all conditions.
solution: Replace the `not { notEquals }` pattern with the `count` operator and a `where` clause: `{"count": {"field": "ipRules[*]", "where": {"allOf": [{"field": "ipRules[*].address", "equals": "10.0.0.1"}, {"field": "ipRules[*].action", "equals": "allow"}]}}, "greaterOrEquals": 1}`. The `count` operator evaluates all conditions per element atomically, preventing cross-element false positives.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureDev/Dev_ARM:/[ARM] Azure Policy/Architecture/Array aliases evaluation

symptom: Azure Policy assignment not taking effect immediately after being created or updated — deny/audit effects not enforced on new resources during the first ~30 minutes after assignment
rootCause: Policy assignments are cached in the ARM layer. The cache is refreshed every 30 minutes. During this window, greenfield (synchronous) policy evaluation uses the stale cached assignments and may not include newly created/updated assignments.
solution: Log out and log back in to Azure portal to force the policy assignment cache to refresh immediately, bypassing the 30-minute wait. Alternatively, wait up to 30 minutes for the cache to refresh automatically. Note: brownfield (compliance scan) results in portal may also take up to 30 minutes due to EventServicesEntries table ingestion delay.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureDev/Dev_ARM:/[ARM] Azure Policy/Architecture/Greenfield vs Brownfield

symptom: Customer cannot find or access the manual policy attestation UI in Azure portal to set compliance state for a `manual` effect policy assignment
rootCause: The attestation UI is only available to Microsoft Defender for Cloud (MDC) premium tier users. Non-premium users do not have access to this UI (Public Preview limitation). Feature is in Public Preview.
solution: Non-premium MDC users must use the Azure REST API (`Microsoft.PolicyInsights/attestations` extension resource) to create/update attestations. Use `Invoke-AzRestMethod` (PowerShell) or `az rest` (CLI). REST API reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/rest/api/policy/attestations — Attestation structure: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/policy/concepts/attestation-structure
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureDev/Dev_ARM:/[ARM] Azure Policy/Architecture/Manual attestation policies

symptom: Customer requests help authoring or troubleshooting Rego policies for AKS or Arc-enabled Kubernetes Azure Policy definitions
rootCause: Rego language is not owned by Microsoft. Azure Policy for Kubernetes leverages Gatekeeper/Rego for enforcement, but Rego policy authoring is out of CSS support scope.
solution: Rego policy authoring is out of CSS scope. Troubleshooting Rego language syntax issues falls under AKS team scope — keep case ownership and open ICM collaboration with AKS team. AKS team may need to escalate to their PG as training has not been fully delivered.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureDev/Dev_ARM:/[ARM] Azure Policy/Architecture/Policy RP integrations/AKS and Azure ARC enabled Kubernetes dataplane policies

symptom: Kubernetes (AKS/Arc) data plane policy compliance results are delayed or not reflecting changes immediately after policy assignment or resource creation/modification
rootCause: Compliance data flows through a multi-step pipeline: Policy pod syncs every 10-15 min → sends to Data Plane service → Geneva ingestion (~5 min) → Rollup worker aggregates in Kusto every 15 min. Total end-to-end delay can be 30-45 minutes.
solution: Wait 30-45 minutes after policy assignment changes for compliance data to propagate. To investigate delays: check Policy pod logs (kubectl), Geneva ActivityCompleted/ActivityFailed logs, and Kusto rollup logs. Reference: LEARN Policy pod logs documentation.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureDev/Dev_ARM:/[ARM] Azure Policy/Architecture/Policy RP integrations/AKS and Azure ARC enabled Kubernetes dataplane policies

symptom: VNETs that meet all conditions for an Azure Virtual Network Manager (AVNM) network group show as 'non-compliant' in Azure Policy — customer is confused about the compliance semantics
rootCause: By design: AVNM uses Policy's non-compliant result as the signal to add VNETs to network groups. A VNET meeting the membership conditions is intentionally flagged non-compliant by Policy so AVNM knows to add it. Policy does not evaluate whether the VNET is already in the group.
solution: This is expected behavior for AVNM-related policy assignments. Non-compliant in this context means 'this VNET should be added to the AVNM network group.' AVNM handles the actual membership assignment accordingly. No remediation is needed for the policy compliance result itself.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureDev/Dev_ARM:/[ARM] Azure Policy/Architecture/Policy RP integrations/Azure Virtual Network Manager

symptom: Policy evaluation details for Key Vault data plane policies are not visible in the Azure Policy Compliance UI — no evaluation reason or breakdown shown
rootCause: Evaluation details are not yet surfaced in the Policy Compliance UI for Key Vault data plane policies. This is a known architecture gap/limitation. Evaluation happens via Key Vault SLM using a Policy nuget library, and details are not piped back to the Policy UI.
solution: Known limitation — evaluation details are not available via the Policy Compliance UI for KV data plane policies. Use Key Vault traces and Kusto logs (Components Greenfield/Brownfield logs, rollup worker data) for investigation. Route Key Vault SLM/nuget issues to Key Vault team via SAPs: Azure/Key Vault/Managing Certificates, Managing Keys, or Managing Secrets.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureDev/Dev_ARM:/[ARM] Azure Policy/Architecture/Policy RP integrations/Key Vault Dataplane policies

symptom: Cannot find Azure Policy RP logs for Machine Learning data plane policy evaluation; standard Policy RP troubleshooting yields no relevant results for ML policy issues
rootCause: ML handles data plane policy evaluation entirely internally via its own policy microservice embedded inside the ML RP. The Azure Policy RP is NOT involved in the evaluation process for ML data plane policies. The policy microservice inside ML RP polls ARM for policy resources every 10 minutes and evaluates payloads locally.
solution: Look for evaluation logs in ML RP traces (ML dataplane endpoint), not Azure Policy RP. Route ML data plane policy issues to ML team via SAP: Azure/Machine Learning/Workspace Management, Configuration and Security/Issues with Azure ML built-in policies. Azure Policy team owns only the policy APIs, definitions, assignments, and UI — not ML's internal evaluation engine.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureDev/Dev_ARM:/[ARM] Azure Policy/Architecture/Policy RP integrations/Machine Learning Asset Stage Management

symptom: Policy compliance results shown in Microsoft Defender for Cloud (MDC) / Security Center (ASC) UI differ from those in the Azure Policy compliance portal
rootCause: MDC uses its own proprietary calculation logic for security posture and compliance scores, which differs from Azure Policy's compliance calculation. MDC also creates its own initiative assignments in the customer's Policy environment to consume policy data. The discrepancy is by design.
solution: This is expected behavior. Azure Policy compliance portal shows Policy RP's view. MDC/ASC UI reflects MDC's own calculation. Direct questions about MDC UI values, scoring differences, or how MDC consumes Policy data to the Microsoft Defender for Cloud team. Azure Policy team scope does not cover MDC UI calculation.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureDev/Dev_ARM:/[ARM] Azure Policy/Architecture/Policy RP integrations/Microsoft Defender for Cloud (formerly known as Azure Security Center (ASC))

symptom: Azure Policy with 'Security Center' category does not appear to directly evaluate the targeted resource; compliance result is unexpected and seems unrelated to actual resource properties
rootCause: Some Security Center category policies use AuditIfNotExists effect targeting Microsoft.Security/assessments resources instead of directly evaluating the resource. Compliance is entirely determined by what the Microsoft.Security RP reports via its assessments API. Example: 'Azure DDoS Protection Standard should be enabled' scans virtualNetworks but delegates the actual compliance decision to Microsoft.Security/assessments with a specific assessment GUID.
solution: For unexpected compliance on 'Security Center' category policies, engage the Microsoft Defender for Cloud team — they own the Microsoft.Security/assessments evaluation that drives the compliance result. Check the policy definition JSON for 'type': 'Microsoft.Security/assessments' in the 'then.details' section to confirm this pattern applies.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureDev/Dev_ARM:/[ARM] Azure Policy/Architecture/Policy RP integrations/Microsoft Defender for Cloud (formerly known as Azure Security Center (ASC))

symptom: Azure Policy aliases: understanding what aliases are, types (scalar vs array), how they map to resource properties — needed for policy definition authoring or debugging incorrect alias usage
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureDev/Dev_ARM:/[ARM] Azure Policy/Architecture/Policy aliases

symptom: Customer hits the Azure Policy definition limit for a subscription or management group and cannot create additional policy definitions
rootCause: Policy definition limits are per-scope (per management group or per subscription) and per-cloud (Public, Fairfax, Mooncake), and cannot be customized. Customers who create one unique definition per resource group exhaust the limit quickly because definitions are not per-resource-group.
solution: Create a single parameterized policy definition and use separate policy assignments per resource group, passing the specific parameter values in each assignment. This pattern multiplies coverage without consuming additional definition quota. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/policy/overview#maximum-count-of-azure-policy-objects
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureDev/Dev_ARM:/[ARM] Azure Policy/Architecture/Policy limits and quotas

symptom: Policy compliance evaluation shows '0 out of 0' results — no resources are scanned or evaluated by the policy
rootCause: The policy definition uses `"mode":"Indexed"` but the target resource type is registered as a proxy resource (not a tracked resource). mode:Indexed only evaluates tracked resources; proxy resource types are bypassed entirely.
solution: Change the policy definition mode to `"mode":"All"`. mode:All evaluates both tracked and proxy resources regardless of the properties they support. Confirm the resource routing type via [[ARCH] Resource routing types] in Dev_ARM wiki.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureDev/Dev_ARM:/[ARM] Azure Policy/Architecture/Policy mode

symptom: Policy targeting Microsoft.Resources/subscriptions or Microsoft.Resources/subscriptions/resourceGroups does not evaluate tags or location correctly when using mode:Indexed
rootCause: Even though mode:Indexed is intended for policies evaluating tags and location, subscription and resource group resource types require mode:All regardless. Using mode:Indexed for these resource types causes them to be skipped.
solution: Always use `"mode":"All"` when the policy target is `Microsoft.Resources/subscriptions` or `Microsoft.Resources/subscriptions/resourceGroups`, even if the policy condition checks for tags or location.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureDev/Dev_ARM:/[ARM] Azure Policy/Architecture/Policy mode

symptom: Customer wants to receive alerts or notifications when Azure Policy compliance state changes, but Policy has no built-in alerting feature
rootCause: Azure Policy does not support creating alerts as built-in functionality out of the box.
solution: Use Azure Event Grid system topic for Azure Policy. Customers can subscribe to compliance state change events via Event Grid and integrate them with Logic Apps, Azure Functions, or other alerting mechanisms. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/policy/concepts/event-overview
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureDev/Dev_ARM:/[ARM] Azure Policy/Policy FAQ

symptom: Customer cannot export Azure Policy compliance data from the Azure Portal
rootCause: Exporting policy compliance data through the Azure Portal is not supported as a built-in portal feature.
solution: Export compliance data using Azure Resource Graph (ARG) queries or via REST API / CLI commands. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/policy/how-to/get-compliance-data
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureDev/Dev_ARM:/[ARM] Azure Policy/Policy FAQ

symptom: An alias is missing for a child property (e.g. parentProperty.childProperty) but the parent property alias exists; customer cannot write a policy targeting the child property
rootCause: The child property alias has not been created. Common reasons: (1) the property is new and not yet onboarded, (2) the parent property is an open/dynamic object so the child cannot be statically defined by the RP, (3) onboarding limitations prevent alias creation.
solution: 1. Confirm alias absence via https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/policy/concepts/definition-structure-alias. 2. Follow the Assistance with Aliases process (ARM TA review) to request alias creation. 3. Workaround if alias cannot be created: target the parent property alias and use `{"value":"[string(field('parentPropertyAlias'))]","contains":"\"childPropertyName\":\"valueToEvaluateAgainst\""}` — this only works in the `if` section of the policy definition.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureDev/Dev_ARM:/[ARM] Azure Policy/Scenarios/Alias does not exist for property, but exists for parent property

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureDev/Dev_ARM:/[ARM] Azure Policy/Process/Support scope and collaboration scenarios

symptom: How to file a supportability request to Azure Policy PG (adding to CSS backlog for triage and development)
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureDev/Dev_ARM:/[ARM] Azure Policy/Process/Supportability request process

symptom: Resource is shown in Azure Policy compliance but portal shows 'Resource Not Found' when clicking on it; resource not visible in All Resources. Occurs when policy assigned to Subscription or Management Group (not Resource Group), for tracked resource types only.
rootCause: ARM cache is out of sync with the resource provider. Policy uses a collection GET directly to the RP (bypassing ARM cache), so it finds the resource. Portal and other clients read from ARM cache which no longer has the resource.
solution: Trigger an ARM cache sync for the impacted resource so ARM re-caches it from the RP. Verify via Jarvis: (1) Get Resources from Provider should show resource exists; (2) Get Resources from Subscription should show resource absent. See ARM cache synchronization TSG.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureDev/Dev_ARM:/[ARM] Azure Policy/Scenarios/Deleted resource is scanned by Policy | Resource not found

symptom: Azure Policy in Indexed mode scans resources that cannot be tagged via Portal; resource supports tags on the RP API but no Tags UI experience is available.
rootCause: Some resource types implement tag support on the RP API but do not provide a resource type Tags UI in the Azure Portal.
solution: Tag the resource via: (1) All Resources blade > Manage view > Show hidden types > select resource > Assign tags; or (2) PowerShell, CLI, or REST API.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureDev/Dev_ARM:/[ARM] Azure Policy/Scenarios/Non taggable resources scanned in indexed mode

symptom: Azure Policy in Indexed mode scans resources that do not actually support tags, resulting in false positive compliance violations.
rootCause: RP team declared routingType=Default in their RP manifest (signaling tags are supported) but did not implement tag support on the resource. Azure Policy uses routingType to determine if a resource type supports tags in Indexed mode.
solution: Real fix is on the RP side: either implement tag support or change routingType to proxyOnly in the RP manifest. Proxy resources are not scanned in Indexed mode. Get the RP manifest via the TSG and verify the routingType property for the resource type.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureDev/Dev_ARM:/[ARM] Azure Policy/Scenarios/Non taggable resources scanned in indexed mode

symptom: Policy compliance results show 0 resources scanned when policy mode is Indexed and the policy definition has a 'type' condition targeting a proxy-only resource type.
rootCause: Indexed mode only scans tracked (non-proxy) resource types. A policy with a 'type' condition targeting a proxy-only resource combined with mode=Indexed results in no resources being found.
solution: Change policy mode from Indexed to All. When a 'type' condition is provided, mode=All scans only the specified resource type regardless of its routing type.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureDev/Dev_ARM:/[ARM] Azure Policy/Scenarios/Policy compliance shows no resources

symptom: Policy compliance shows 0 resources scanned; policy mode and scope appear correct but no resources are evaluated.
rootCause: No resources in the assignment scope match the policy definition's applicability criteria. Resources are only scanned when they match the definition's 'name', 'type', and 'kind' conditions, which act as filters for the policy engine to determine applicability.
solution: (1) Create a resource that meets the applicability criteria; (2) modify the policy definition to broaden the 'name'/'type'/'kind' conditions; or (3) change the assignment scope to where applicable resources exist.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureDev/Dev_ARM:/[ARM] Azure Policy/Scenarios/Policy compliance shows no resources

symptom: Policy compliance shows 0 resources or incorrect compliance state despite applicable resources existing in the assignment scope and policy mode being correct.
rootCause: Assignment has notScopes (exclusions) configured that cover the applicable resources, or policy exemptions exist at the same or sub-scope, effectively hiding them from compliance evaluation.
solution: Review the assignment notScopes and remove incorrect exclusions. Review and remove incorrect exemptions impacting the scope or sub-scope where compliance is expected.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureDev/Dev_ARM:/[ARM] Azure Policy/Scenarios/Policy compliance shows no resources

symptom: ARM template containing a Policy definition or initiative fails to deploy; template engine misinterprets policy functions as ARM template functions causing deployment errors.
rootCause: Both ARM templates and Azure Policy use square bracket syntax [function()] for functions. When a policy definition/initiative is embedded in an ARM template, the template engine cannot distinguish between policy functions and template functions, processing policy functions incorrectly.
solution: Escape policy functions within the ARM template using double opening bracket: use '[[function()]]' for policy functions you do NOT want the template engine to process, and '[function()]' for actual ARM template functions that SHOULD be processed.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureDev/Dev_ARM:/[ARM] Azure Policy/Scenarios/Policy deployment fails

symptom: Customer applied an allowed-resource-types policy; creation of a resource type explicitly included in the allow list fails with a policy deny error. The deployment page does not clearly show which resource type was actually denied.
rootCause: Creating one resource type can trigger automatic creation of a dependent child resource with a different type and a different correlationId. That child resource type is not included in the allow list. The deployment error message does not accurately reflect the denied resource type.
solution: Search EventServiceEntries Kusto table (armprod.kusto.windows.net/ARMProd) with the original correlationId to find the entity name, then search by entity name to find the actual denied resource type with its own correlationId. Add that child resource type to the policy allow list.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureDev/Dev_ARM:/[ARM] Azure Policy/Scenarios/Resource type being denied despite being allowed in the policy

symptom: Microsoft.Sql/servers/databases resource named 'master' is not scanned by Azure Policy (for Audit/Deny/other effects), even when the policy definition targets this resource type.
rootCause: By design: SQL team does not allow Azure users to modify the master database. Azure Policy PG has excluded master DB from evaluation for all subscriptions for non-AINE/DINE effects. AINE and DINE policies still scan master DB.
solution: No solution available - this is by design. If customer has a valid scenario requiring master DB scanning under other effects, contact the ARM Policy SME Teams channel.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureDev/Dev_ARM:/[ARM] Azure Policy/Scenarios/SQL master DB not scanned

symptom: Azure Policy service updates and feature changes: DenyAction GA, exemptions in ARG, export to GitHub deprecated, selectors/overrides, manual attestation, UAMI support, AKS custom policies, remediation task enhancements.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureDev/Dev_ARM:/[ARM] Azure Policy/Service Updates

symptom: Microsoft.Insights/diagnosticSettings extension resource cannot be enabled on Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines via Azure Portal (only the VM diagnostic extension agent is available via Portal).
rootCause: The classic diagnosticSettings (Microsoft.Insights/diagnosticSettings extension resource) is not exposed in Portal for VMs. Only the VM diagnostic extension (which installs an agent on the VM) is available via the Portal experience.
solution: Enable Microsoft.Insights/diagnosticSettings for VMs via PowerShell or CLI only. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/essentials/diagnostic-settings?tabs=CMD#create-using-powershell
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureDev/Dev_ARM:/[ARM] Azure Policy/Special RP considerations

symptom: Azure Policy scan jobs for Microsoft.Logic/workflows do not execute in certain Microsoft tenants (72f988bf-86f1-41af-91ab-2d7cd011db47, 33e01921-4d64-4f8c-a055-5bdaffd5e33d, 975f013f-7f24-47e8-a7d3-abc4752bf346), making it impossible to test policy definitions for Logic Apps in those tenants.
rootCause: Policy scan jobs for Microsoft.Logic/workflows have been deliberately disabled in Microsoft tenants due to scalability issues caused by excessive workflow resource scan volume.
solution: Use a non-Microsoft tenant to test or validate policy definitions targeting Microsoft.Logic/workflows. Inform customer/engineers that this is expected behavior in Microsoft tenants.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureDev/Dev_ARM:/[ARM] Azure Policy/Special RP considerations

symptom: Azure Policy to block NSG security rule creation is only partially effective: rules can still be created through one path even when the policy targets Microsoft.Network/networkSecurityGroups/securityRules.
rootCause: securityRules exists both as a child resource type (Microsoft.Network/networkSecurityGroups/securityRules) and as a property on the parent NSG resource. Rules can be created via either path; a policy targeting only one path will not block the other.
solution: Write the policy to target both: (1) Microsoft.Network/networkSecurityGroups (block the securityRules property on the parent) AND (2) Microsoft.Network/networkSecurityGroups/securityRules (block the child resource creation directly).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureDev/Dev_ARM:/[ARM] Azure Policy/Special RP considerations

symptom: PowerPlatform resources (e.g., Microsoft.PowerPlatform resource types) are blocked by an allowed-locations policy even when deploying to valid PowerPlatform regions such as 'unitedstates' or 'unitedkingdom'. Logical regions do not appear in the locations parameter dropdown in Portal.
rootCause: PowerPlatform resource types use logical regions (not standard physical Azure regions). The Azure Policy locations strongType hides logical regions by default, so they are not available in the Portal-based parameter assignment UI.
solution: Add logical region values (e.g., 'unitedstates', 'unitedkingdom') directly to the policy parameter value via CLI, PowerShell, or REST API (not via Portal). Alternatively, duplicate the policy definition and remove the strongType to allow manual entry of parameter values.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureDev/Dev_ARM:/[ARM] Azure Policy/Special RP considerations

symptom: Azure Policy evaluates null values for properties on Microsoft.Web/sites during partial updates, causing unexpected non-compliance or policy enforcement failures for App Service resources.
rootCause: Microsoft.Web/sites uses PUT instead of PATCH for some partial resource changes. Policy expects a full payload on PUT; partial PUT requests omit properties, causing Policy to see them as null.
solution: Use an append+deny policy pattern: the append effect adds the required property with expected value if it is missing (null), while the deny effect blocks if the property exists with the wrong value. This mitigates the partial PUT issue for fixed-value properties on Microsoft.Web/sites.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureDev/Dev_ARM:/[ARM] Azure Policy/Special RP considerations

symptom: AINE/Audit policy targeting siteConfig properties on Microsoft.Web/sites always shows inconsistent or non-compliant state for existing (brownfield) App Service resources, even when settings are correctly configured.
rootCause: siteConfig properties return null on a GET operation for the Microsoft.Web/sites resource type (even when correctly configured on the actual resource), making policy compliance always appear inconsistent for brownfield scenarios.
solution: For brownfield compliance, write a separate AINE policy targeting the Microsoft.Web/sites/config child resource type instead of siteConfig on the parent. Using siteConfig on the parent Microsoft.Web/sites only works reliably for Greenfield scenarios (new resource creation).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureDev/Dev_ARM:/[ARM] Azure Policy/Special RP considerations

symptom: Azure 资源操作被 Azure Policy 阻止，报错 RequestDisallowedByPolicy（HTTP 403）
rootCause: 资源的属性不满足 deny 策略的条件，策略评估触发 deny effect
solution: 从错误响应的 error.additionalInfo[0].info 中获取 policyDefinitionId 和 policyAssignmentId，evaluatedExpressions 中显示具体哪些条件判断为 True 导致触发；若用户无 Policy 读权限则 evaluationDetails 字段被省略但 assignment/definition ID 仍存在；如果操作本不应被阻止，转入 Policy enforcement not as expected TSG
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureDev/Dev_ARM:/[ARM] Azure Policy/Support Topics/Operation blocked by Policy/Operation blocked by Policy
sourceUrl: https://dev.azure.com/Supportability/AzureDev/_wiki/wikis/Dev_ARM?pagePath=/[ARM] Azure Policy/Support Topics/Operation blocked by Policy/Operation blocked by Policy

symptom: DINE (deployIfNotExists) 策略合规评估不正确：同一 RG 中任何同类型资源都能让评估通过，导致误报为 compliant
rootCause: if.field.type 与 then.details.type 相同时未指定 then.details.name，导致 Policy 对整个 RG 的同类型资源做 Collection GET，任意资源都能满足 existenceCondition
solution: 为 then.details.name 指定值 "[field('fullname')]"，使 Policy 只针对当前资源执行 GET 评估
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureDev/Dev_ARM:/[ARM] Azure Policy/Troubleshooting Guides/AINE and DINE checklist
sourceUrl: https://dev.azure.com/Supportability/AzureDev/_wiki/wikis/Dev_ARM?pagePath=/[ARM] Azure Policy/Troubleshooting Guides/AINE and DINE checklist

symptom: AINE/DINE 策略对 diagnosticSettings、roleAssignment 等订阅级别资源评估失败或范围错误（默认在 resourceGroup 范围查找）
rootCause: existenceScope 默认值为 resourceGroup，对订阅级别的资源（如 diagnosticSettings、roleAssignments）未设置为 subscription
solution: 在 then.details 中设置 existenceScope 为 subscription
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureDev/Dev_ARM:/[ARM] Azure Policy/Troubleshooting Guides/AINE and DINE checklist
sourceUrl: https://dev.azure.com/Supportability/AzureDev/_wiki/wikis/Dev_ARM?pagePath=/[ARM] Azure Policy/Troubleshooting Guides/AINE and DINE checklist

symptom: AINE/DINE 策略合规评估很慢甚至超时（evaluation timeout）
rootCause: name 和 type 条件定义在 existenceCondition 中而非 then.details.type/name，导致 Policy 执行 Collection GET 并对所有返回资源逐一评估，性能损耗大
solution: 将 name 移到 then.details.name，将 type 移到 then.details.type，而非放入 existenceCondition；then.details 中的 type/name 用于缩小 GET 请求范围为 targeted GET
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureDev/Dev_ARM:/[ARM] Azure Policy/Troubleshooting Guides/AINE and DINE checklist
sourceUrl: https://dev.azure.com/Supportability/AzureDev/_wiki/wikis/Dev_ARM?pagePath=/[ARM] Azure Policy/Troubleshooting Guides/AINE and DINE checklist

symptom: DINE 策略的 remediation deployment 在订阅级别部署时失败，报缺少 location 或 template schema 错误
rootCause: 订阅级别部署需要在 then.details.deployment 中提供 location 属性，且 ARM template schema 必须是订阅部署格式；deploymentScope 未设为 subscription
solution: 设置 then.details.deploymentScope 为 subscription；在 then.details.deployment 中添加 location 属性；确认 ARM template 使用订阅级别的 schema 结构
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureDev/Dev_ARM:/[ARM] Azure Policy/Troubleshooting Guides/AINE and DINE checklist
sourceUrl: https://dev.azure.com/Supportability/AzureDev/_wiki/wikis/Dev_ARM?pagePath=/[ARM] Azure Policy/Troubleshooting Guides/AINE and DINE checklist

symptom: Policy 条件评估失败：被评估的属性在资源 payload 中不存在（PUT/PATCH 时 RP 使用默认值），导致合规判断异常
rootCause: RP 在 PUT/PATCH 时不强制要求所有属性存在，缺失属性使用默认值；Policy 条件未处理属性不存在的情况
solution: 添加 exists 或 notExists 条件判断属性是否存在；参考 Azure REST API Reference 确认属性默认值；明确默认值对应的期望合规状态，转化为 exists/notExists 条件逻辑
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureDev/Dev_ARM:/[ARM] Azure Policy/Troubleshooting Guides/Condition checklist

symptom: Policy 条件评估结果不正确，表面上值匹配但条件仍返回不符合预期结果（如 true 不等于 "true"，20 不等于 "20"）
rootCause: 类型不匹配：boolean true 与 string "true" 不同，integer 20 与 string "20" 不同，array 与 object 不同；Policy 无法跨类型比较
solution: 确认 RP 返回的属性类型（boolean/integer/string）；确保 policy 条件使用相同类型的值；对 append 策略检查 array vs object；使用 Get-AzPolicyAlias 或 ARM REST API 获取实际属性类型
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureDev/Dev_ARM:/[ARM] Azure Policy/Troubleshooting Guides/Condition checklist

symptom: Policy 评估出现 exception/error，资源被标记为 error 状态而非 compliant/non-compliant，常见于条件中使用了 split()、substring() 等函数
rootCause: 函数在特定输入下返回 null 或数组越界：如 split(field('name'),'-')[1] 当 name 中无连字符时访问 null 导致异常
solution: 在函数调用前加 if() 防护：如 if(greaterOrEquals(length(split(field('name'),'-')),2), split(field('name'),'-')[1], 'fallback')；所有数组索引访问前先 length 检查
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureDev/Dev_ARM:/[ARM] Azure Policy/Troubleshooting Guides/Condition checklist

symptom: ARG query returns stale/outdated data — resource state changed outside of ARM (data plane or non-ARM path) and change is not reflected in ARG; delay can be up to ~30 hours
rootCause: Azure Resource Graph updates its cache only on ARM PUT/PATCH/DELETE operations. State changes that bypass ARM (e.g. data plane writes) are not detected by ARG until the next full scan, which runs approximately every 30 hours.
solution: Short-term: retrieve current resource state via direct RP APIs. Long-term: file feature request for the RP to implement ARG state-change notifications. If notifications may already be in place but broken, open IcM to ARG PG for investigation. Customer can also wait ~30 hours for the scheduled full scan.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureDev/Dev_ARM:/[ARM] Azure Resource Graph/Scenarios/Stale data in ARG query results

symptom: ARG query returns stale or missing data for proxy resource types (e.g. securityResources, policyResources, or other non-standard ARG tables); ARG data differs from what RP APIs return
rootCause: Proxy resource types rely on a custom ingestion pipeline between the RP and ARG. If this pipeline is degraded or broken, ARG data will lag behind or diverge from RP API data.
solution: 1. Confirm root: call the RP API directly — if RP data is also outdated, issue is RP-side, not ARG. 2. If only ARG is stale: open IcM to ARG PG to investigate the ingestion pipeline for that proxy resource type. Discuss scenario with TA/PTA before filing.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureDev/Dev_ARM:/[ARM] Azure Resource Graph/Scenarios/Stale data in ARG query results

symptom: ARM API calls return HTTP 429 throttling errors, engineer needs to determine if throttling is from ARM layer or from the resource provider
rootCause: ARM and RP throttling are separate layers; ARM rate-limits incoming requests before forwarding to RP; they show up in different Kusto tables
solution: Check HttpIncomingRequests table for HTTP 429 responses to identify ARM-level throttling; check HttpOutgoingRequests table for HTTP 429 to identify RP-level throttling. 429 in Incoming = ARM throttled the call before it reached the RP; 429 in Outgoing = RP throttled the forwarded call.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureDev/Dev_ARM:/Training/[Trainer only] ARM foundations training outline

symptom: ARM API calls are slow or have unexpectedly high latency; unclear whether delay originates from ARM processing or from the resource provider backend
rootCause: ARM adds processing time before forwarding to RP; HttpIncomingRequests.durationInMilliseconds captures total end-to-end duration (ARM + RP); HttpOutgoingRequests.durationInMilliseconds captures only the RP portion
solution: Compare durationInMilliseconds in HttpIncomingRequests (total) vs HttpOutgoingRequests (RP time) using the same correlationId. The delta (Incoming minus Outgoing) indicates ARM-side delay; the Outgoing value indicates RP-side delay. Use this to determine which side to escalate.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureDev/Dev_ARM:/Training/[Trainer only] ARM foundations training outline

symptom: Customer reports issues with Azure Blueprints (assignment failures, definition errors) or asks about the future of Blueprints service
rootCause: Azure Blueprints is on a deprecation path and will be phased out; customers should plan migration
solution: Recommend migration to Template Specs and Deployment Stacks. For active Blueprints bugs or PG escalations, file IcM via https://aka.ms/Blueprint-ICM to Azure Deployments team. Blueprints supports Microsoft.Blueprint resource provider including assignments, definitions, artifacts, and deny assignments.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureDev/Dev_ARM:/[ARM] Azure Blueprint/Start Here

symptom: Customer moved a subscription with active Lighthouse delegation to another tenant and is uncertain about delegation behavior post-migration
rootCause: Subscription tenant migration has different effects on Lighthouse delegations depending on whether the target tenant is one of the existing managedBy tenants
solution: If target tenant IS one of the managedBy tenants: Registration Assignments, Registration Definitions, and RBAC roles related to that target tenant are removed; delegations to other tenants remain intact. If target tenant is NOT a managedBy tenant: All active delegations remain in place and continue to work normally after the tenant migration.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureDev/Dev_ARM:/[ARM] Azure Lighthouse/Lighthouse FAQ

symptom: Policy using allOf with multiple `not { field: 'array[*].propertyA', notEquals: 'val1' }` and `not { field: 'array[*].propertyB', notEquals: 'val2' }` conditions returns false positives; policy passes even when no single array element satisfies both conditions simultaneously
rootCause: Array aliases evaluate each condition independently across all elements; the two conditions can be satisfied by two different elements of the same array, not necessarily the same element — a fundamental behavioral difference from non-array alias conditions
solution: Use the `count` operator with a `where` clause containing an `allOf` block to enforce that all conditions are evaluated on the same array element: `{ "count": { "field": "array[*]", "where": { "allOf": [ { "field": "array[*].propertyA", "equals": "val1" }, { "field": "array[*].propertyB", "equals": "val2" } ] } }, "greater": 0 }`
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureDev/Dev_ARM:/[ARM] Azure Policy/Architecture/Array aliases evaluation

symptom: Newly assigned Azure Policy not enforcing (not blocking/auditing resources) on PUT or PATCH requests within 30 minutes of assignment creation; policy appears assigned but has no effect on resource operations
rootCause: Policy assignments are cached at the ARM layer; the cache is refreshed approximately every 30 minutes; until the cache is refreshed, the new assignment is not picked up during greenfield evaluation
solution: Log out and log back in to Azure portal or re-authenticate the API client to force the ARM policy cache to refresh immediately; alternatively wait up to 30 minutes for the cache to refresh automatically
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureDev/Dev_ARM:/[ARM] Azure Policy/Architecture/Greenfield vs Brownfield

symptom: Compliance scan results not updated in Azure portal for up to 30 minutes after policy assignment creation or after a resource is modified; compliance state appears stale or does not reflect recent changes
rootCause: Brownfield scan is triggered via EventServicesEntries table entries ingested from Geneva; this ingestion pipeline can take up to 30 minutes; this affects both post-assignment full scans and per-resource scans triggered after greenfield events
solution: Wait up to 30 minutes for compliance state to update automatically; for immediate results trigger an on-demand evaluation scan via Azure portal (Policy > Compliance > Re-evaluate) or via API/CLI: `az policy state trigger-scan`
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureDev/Dev_ARM:/[ARM] Azure Policy/Architecture/Greenfield vs Brownfield

symptom: AuditIfNotExists (AINE) or DeployIfNotExists (DINE) policy does not trigger existence check or auto-remediation deployment immediately after a resource is created or updated via PUT/PATCH; policy seems to have no effect right after resource provisioning
rootCause: AINE/DINE existence check is intentionally scheduled 10 minutes after initial greenfield evaluation by design; the platform first evaluates the if-section conditions synchronously, then schedules a deferred existence check 10 minutes later (configurable via `evaluationDelay` property)
solution: Wait 10 minutes after resource creation/update for AINE/DINE to trigger; if a different delay is needed, configure the `evaluationDelay` property in the policy definition's details section to a custom ISO 8601 duration; remediation tasks can also be used to trigger DINE deployments manually
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureDev/Dev_ARM:/[ARM] Azure Policy/Architecture/How control plane effects work

symptom: Resources show 'Unknown' or 'NonCompliant' compliance state for manual effect policies even though the relevant compliance requirement is considered met; no resource configuration issue exists
rootCause: Manual policies require an explicit attestation resource (Microsoft.PolicyInsights/attestations) to set compliance state; without an attestation, the default state is either 'Unknown' or 'NonCompliant' depending on the definition's defaultComplianceState configuration; compliance is not determined by brownfield scan for manual policies
solution: Create an attestation resource for the applicable scope (resource/resource group/subscription): (1) MDC UI — available only to Microsoft Defender for Cloud Premium tier users; (2) REST API — use PowerShell `Invoke-AzRestMethod` or Azure CLI `az rest` to POST to the attestations API; see https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/policy/concepts/attestation-structure for attestation payload structure
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureDev/Dev_ARM:/[ARM] Azure Policy/Architecture/Manual attestation policies

symptom: Azure Policy control plane behavior reference: how each policy effect (Audit, Deny, Append, Modify, AINE, DINE, Manual, DenyAction) triggers GET operations and handles enforcement on greenfield (PUT/PATCH) vs brownfield (compliance scan)
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureDev/Dev_ARM:/[ARM] Azure Policy/Architecture/How control plane effects work

symptom: 'Security Center' 类别的 Policy 策略显示意外的合规结果（例如 DDoS 已启用但策略仍报 non-compliant）
rootCause: 这类策略使用 AuditIfNotExists effect，其 details.type 为 Microsoft.Security/assessments，而非直接评估目标资源属性。合规性完全由 Microsoft.Security RP 的 assessment 数据决定，与 DDoS/资源本身配置无关。
solution: 将此类合规问题移交 Microsoft Defender for Cloud 团队，由其核查对应 subscription 下 Microsoft.Security/assessments 资源的 status.code。Policy 侧无法影响这类合规结果。SAP: Microsoft Defender for Cloud 相关 SAP。
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureDev/Dev_ARM:/[ARM] Azure Policy/Architecture/Policy RP integrations/Microsoft Defender for Cloud (formerly known as Azure Security Center (ASC))

symptom: MDfC (ASC) 界面显示的合规分数/状态与 Azure Policy Compliance 界面不一致
rootCause: MDfC 在 Azure Policy 合规数据之上有自己的计算逻辑，两者结果存在差异属预期行为。
solution: 这是 by design。Azure Policy 侧的数据是正确的；MDfC UI 中的展示由 MDfC 团队负责，任何关于 MDfC 界面计算方式的疑问需由 MDfC 团队解答。
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureDev/Dev_ARM:/[ARM] Azure Policy/Architecture/Policy RP integrations/Microsoft Defender for Cloud (formerly known as Azure Security Center (ASC))

symptom: AVNM 网络组成员资格与 Policy 合规状态的对应关系令人困惑：满足条件的 VNET 反而显示 non-compliant，但被加入了网络组
rootCause: AVNM 与 Policy 的集成使用了反向逻辑：Policy 将满足所有条件的 VNET 标记为 non-compliant，AVNM 读取 non-compliant 结果后将该 VNET 加入网络组。这是 by design。
solution: 向客户解释：AVNM 场景下 Policy non-compliant = '应加入网络组'，compliant = '不应加入'。这是 AVNM 与 Policy 集成的设计行为，非 Bug。AVNM 的后续处理（加入/移除网络组）由 AVNM 团队负责。
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureDev/Dev_ARM:/[ARM] Azure Policy/Architecture/Policy RP integrations/Azure Virtual Network Manager

symptom: AKS/Arc Kubernetes 的 Policy 合规数据更新缓慢或显示陈旧结果
rootCause: K8s Policy 合规数据经过多层异步管道：Policy pod 每 10 分钟拉取策略 + Gatekeeper 每 10 分钟扫描 + Policy pod 每 15 分钟上报数据至 Data Plane service + Geneva→Kusto 入库约 5 分钟 + Rollup worker 每 15 分钟聚合。全流程最长约 55 分钟延迟。
solution: 告知客户 K8s 策略合规数据最长有 ~55 分钟延迟，属正常行为。排查步骤：1) 检查 Policy pod 日志（Azure Policy addon pod 归属 AKS/Arc 团队）；2) 检查 Data Plane service ActivityCompleted/ActivityFailed 日志；3) 查 Kusto 中 Components Brownfield logs；4) 如果数据完全缺失超过 1 小时，考虑切 ICM 至 Azure Policy/Dataplane。
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureDev/Dev_ARM:/[ARM] Azure Policy/Architecture/Policy RP integrations/AKS and Azure ARC enabled Kubernetes dataplane policies

symptom: Key Vault dataplane policy 的合规详情（evaluation details）在 Policy Compliance UI 中不可见/无法查看
rootCause: 这是已知功能限制：当前 evaluation details 尚未在 Policy Compliance UI 中为 Key Vault dataplane policies 实现。
solution: 告知客户这是当前平台限制，evaluation details 对 Key Vault dataplane policies 不可用。如需排查合规原因，需借助 Key Vault traces 和 Kusto 中的 Components Greenfield/Brownfield logs（需 Key Vault 团队协助）。
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureDev/Dev_ARM:/[ARM] Azure Policy/Architecture/Policy RP integrations/Key Vault Dataplane policies

symptom: Customer hits Azure Policy definition limit at management group or subscription scope; cannot create additional policy definitions
rootCause: Definition limits are per-scope (per management group or per subscription). Customers who create one definition per resource group exhaust the per-subscription limit quickly instead of using parameterized reusable definitions.
solution: Create a single parameterized policy definition and create one assignment per resource group with different parameter values, rather than creating separate definitions per resource group. This way X definitions at MG level + X definitions at subscription level can cover hundreds of resource-group-level assignments.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureDev/Dev_ARM:/[ARM] Azure Policy/Architecture/Policy limits and quotas

symptom: Policy compliance scan shows '0 out of 0' applicable resources for a resource type that should be evaluated
rootCause: Policy definition mode is set to 'Indexed', which only evaluates tracked resources. Proxy resource types are bypassed entirely under Indexed mode, resulting in no scan results.
solution: Change the policy definition mode from 'Indexed' to 'All'. Use 'All' when the policy needs to evaluate both tracked and proxy resource types. Note: always use mode 'All' for Microsoft.Resources/subscriptions and Microsoft.Resources/subscriptions/resourceGroups even if the policy checks for tags or location.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureDev/Dev_ARM:/[ARM] Azure Policy/Architecture/Policy mode

symptom: Customer wants to export Azure Policy compliance data but cannot find the option in the Azure portal
solution: Compliance data export through the portal is not supported. Use Azure Resource Graph (ARG) or REST API / CLI to export compliance data. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/policy/how-to/get-compliance-data
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureDev/Dev_ARM:/[ARM] Azure Policy/Policy FAQ

symptom: Customer wants to create alerts based on Azure Policy compliance state changes but Policy does not have built-in alert functionality
solution: Azure Policy does not support built-in alerts. Use Event Grid system topic: Policy is supported as an Event Grid system topic. Subscribe to compliance state change events and integrate with alerting/automation solutions. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/policy/concepts/event-overview
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureDev/Dev_ARM:/[ARM] Azure Policy/Policy FAQ

symptom: Alias is missing for child property '{parentProperty}.{childProperty}' but exists for parent property; unable to author a policy targeting the specific child property
rootCause: Alias has not been created for the child property. Common reasons: (1) Property is newly added, (2) Parent property is an open object so child cannot be formally defined by RP, (3) Property cannot be onboarded due to certain limitations.
solution: 1) Confirm alias existence via https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/policy/concepts/definition-structure-alias. 2) Request alias creation following SAP Assistance with Aliases process. 3) If alias cannot be created, use this workaround in the 'if' section only (does not work elsewhere): {"value":"[string(field('parentPropertyAlias'))]","contains":"\"childPropertyName\":\"valueToEvaluateAgainst\""}. This targets the parent property alias using a string contains check.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureDev/Dev_ARM:/[ARM] Azure Policy/Scenarios/Alias does not exist for property, but exists for parent property

symptom: Policy compliance 中某资源显示 'Resource Not Found'，点击资源时 Portal 报错。资源不出现在 All Resources 视图中，但仍被 Policy 扫描。仅在 Policy 分配到 Subscription 或 Management Group 时出现，分配到 Resource Group 不受影响。
rootCause: ARM 缓存与资源提供程序 (RP) 不同步。Policy 使用 Collection GET 直接查询 RP（绕过 ARM 缓存），RP 仍有该资源记录；而 Portal 等客户端通过 ARM 缓存访问，缓存已过期，导致 'Resource Not Found'。
solution: 在 JARVIS Jarvis Actions 中触发 ARM cache synchronization：1) Get Resources from Provider 验证资源在 RP 存在；2) Get Resources from Subscription 验证资源不在 ARM 缓存；3) 执行 ARM cache sync 操作。参考 [TSG] ARM cache synchronization。
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureDev/Dev_ARM:/[ARM] Azure Policy/Scenarios/Deleted resource is scanned by Policy | Resource not found

symptom: Azure Policy 在 Indexed 模式下扫描了无法打标签的资源，客户认为该资源不应被扫描。
rootCause: 某些资源类型在 RP API 层面支持 tags，但 Portal 中没有提供 Tags UI。这导致用户认为该资源不可标签化，而 Policy 在 Indexed 模式下仍然扫描它（因为 routingType 为 Default）。
solution: 方案1：通过 All Resources 页面标签：进入 All Resources → Manage view → Show hidden types → 选择资源 → Assign tags。方案2：通过 PowerShell、CLI 或 REST API 打标签。参考文档：https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-resource-manager/management/tag-resources
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureDev/Dev_ARM:/[ARM] Azure Policy/Scenarios/Non taggable resources scanned in indexed mode

symptom: Azure Policy 在 Indexed 模式下扫描了不支持 tags 的资源（RP 侧未实现 tag 支持但 manifest 声明为 Default routingType）。
rootCause: 资源提供程序在 RP manifest 的 resourceTypes 中将 routingType 设为 Default（表示支持 tags），但实际未实现 tag 功能。Azure Policy 依据 routingType 判断 Indexed 模式下的扫描范围，从而产生误扫。这是 RP 违反 RPC 规范的问题。
solution: 根本解决方案在 RP 侧：RP 团队需要实现 tags 支持，或将该资源类型的 routingType 改为 proxyOnly（Proxy 类型资源在 Indexed 模式下不被扫描）。可通过 [TSG] Get RP manifest 获取 manifest 并检查 routingType。临时方案：将 policy mode 改为 All 并加 type 条件过滤。
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureDev/Dev_ARM:/[ARM] Azure Policy/Scenarios/Non taggable resources scanned in indexed mode

symptom: Policy 合规结果显示 0 个资源被扫描（compliant/non-compliant 均为 0）。Policy 定义中有 type 条件指向某个 proxy-only 资源类型，mode 设为 Indexed。
rootCause: Policy mode 设为 Indexed 时，只扫描 tracked 资源（routingType=Default）。如果 policy 定义中的 type 条件指向 proxy-only 资源，Indexed 模式不会扫描这类资源，导致 0 结果。
solution: 将 policy mode 从 Indexed 改为 All。当 policy 有明确的 type 条件时，mode 设为 All 也不会扩大扫描范围（type 条件本身起过滤作用），因此无副作用。
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureDev/Dev_ARM:/[ARM] Azure Policy/Scenarios/Policy compliance shows no resources

symptom: Policy 合规结果显示 0 个资源被扫描，Policy 定义的 mode 和 scope 配置看起来正常。
rootCause: scope 内没有满足 policy 定义 applicability 条件（name / type / kind）的资源。Policy engine 使用这三个条件确定资源的适用性，如果 scope 内无匹配资源则扫描结果为空。
solution: 三种解法：1) 在 scope 内创建满足 applicability 条件的资源；2) 修改 policy 定义的适用性条件使其匹配现有资源；3) 将 assignment scope 改为有匹配资源的 scope。
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureDev/Dev_ARM:/[ARM] Azure Policy/Scenarios/Policy compliance shows no resources

symptom: Policy 合规结果显示 0 个资源被扫描，但 scope 内确实存在符合条件的资源，policy mode 和 type 条件也正确。
rootCause: Assignment 配置了 notScopes 排除了子 scope，或者相关资源/scope 存在 exemption，导致资源被排除在扫描范围外。
solution: 检查 assignment 的 notScopes 字段，移除不正确的排除配置；检查并删除对该 scope 或子 scope 不正确的 exemption。
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureDev/Dev_ARM:/[ARM] Azure Policy/Scenarios/Policy compliance shows no resources

symptom: 部署包含 Policy 资源（definition/initiative）的 ARM 模板失败，错误提示模板函数无法解析。
rootCause: ARM 模板引擎无法区分属于 policy 的函数和属于模板本身的函数，因为两者都使用 `[function()]` 语法（方括号包裹）。模板引擎会尝试解析 policy definition 内的函数，导致解析错误。
solution: 在 ARM 模板中，所有 policy 定义/initiative 内部需要 policy engine 而非模板引擎处理的函数，使用 `[[function()]` 双方括号转义。规则：需要模板引擎处理的用 `[...]`，需要 policy engine 处理的用 `[[...`（双方括号，仅开头）。
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureDev/Dev_ARM:/[ARM] Azure Policy/Scenarios/Policy deployment fails

symptom: 客户配置了仅允许特定资源类型的 Policy，但某个在允许列表中的资源类型的创建操作仍然失败，且 Portal 部署页面未显示准确的被拒绝资源类型。
rootCause: 创建某个资源类型时，会触发另一个资源类型的自动创建（使用不同的 correlationId）。被拒绝的是这个隐式创建的子资源类型，而非客户直接创建的资源类型，导致错误信息不直观。
solution: 1) 用 EventServiceEntries Kusto 查询 + 原始 correlationId 定位所有相关 PUT 操作；2) 找到实际被 deny 的资源类型（entityName）；3) 将该资源类型加入 policy 允许列表。Kusto 查询：`EventServiceEntries | where subscriptionId == subid | where correlationId == correlationid`
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureDev/Dev_ARM:/[ARM] Azure Policy/Scenarios/Resource type being denied despite being allowed in the policy

symptom: Microsoft.Sql/servers/databases 中 name 为 master 的数据库未被 Azure Policy 扫描（AINE 和 DINE 效果除外）。
rootCause: Azure Policy PG 将 SQL master DB 从所有订阅的 policy 评估中排除（除 AINE 和 DINE 效果外），因为客户无法修改 master DB 使其合规，评估无意义。这是 by design 的行为。
solution: 此为设计行为，无解决方案。注意：master DB 仍会被 AINE（Audit If Not Exists）和 DINE（Deploy If Not Exists）效果的 policy 扫描。如客户认为有特殊场景需要扫描 master DB，联系 ARM Policy SME channel。
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureDev/Dev_ARM:/[ARM] Azure Policy/Scenarios/SQL master DB not scanned

symptom: 需要了解 Azure Policy 近期服务更新，包括 DenyAction GA、Policy exemptions in ARG、Selectors/Overrides、Manual attestation、UAMI 支持等功能变更。

symptom: 客户无法找到所需资源属性对应的 Policy alias，或需要协助排查 alias 是否存在、是否为 Data Plane 属性。
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureDev/Dev_ARM:/[ARM] Azure Policy/Support Topics/Authoring a Policy/Assistance with aliases

symptom: 创建或更新资源时收到 RequestDisallowedByPolicy 错误，操作被 Azure Policy 拒绝
rootCause: 资源违反了 Deny 效果的策略定义，错误消息中包含触发的 policyAssignment 和 policyDefinition 信息
solution: 1. 读取错误消息中的 policyIdentifiers 字段，定位具体的策略赋值名称和 ID；2. 检查 evaluationDetails.evaluatedExpressions，确认哪些属性值不满足策略条件；3. 修改资源属性以符合策略要求，或联系 Policy 管理员排除豁免
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureDev/Dev_ARM:/[ARM] Azure Policy/Support Topics/Operation blocked by Policy/Operation blocked by Policy

symptom: RequestDisallowedByPolicy 错误中缺少 evaluationDetails 详情，无法得知具体哪个条件不满足
rootCause: 发起操作的用户没有 Policy 相关权限，策略引擎会从错误响应中移除 evaluationDetails，仅返回 policyAssignment 和 policyDefinition 标识
solution: 通知用户联系其 Security 团队或 Policy 管理员；工程师不应代替客户修改租户策略配置。如果客户认为操作不应被拒绝，引导其至 [Policy enforcement not as expected] 排查路径
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureDev/Dev_ARM:/[ARM] Azure Policy/Support Topics/Operation blocked by Policy/Operation blocked by Policy

symptom: 资源合规状态不符合预期（brownfield 扫描结果意外合规/非合规），或扫描范围不正确
rootCause: 策略定义的 mode 设置不正确，导致资源类型不在评估范围内
solution: 检查策略 mode 设置：Indexed 模式仅评估支持 tags 和 location 的资源类型；All 模式评估所有资源类型。根据目标资源类型选择正确 mode
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureDev/Dev_ARM:/[ARM] Azure Policy/Support Topics/Policy behavior not as expected/Compliance state and details not as expected

symptom: 使用数组别名 ([*]) 的策略合规状态不符合预期
rootCause: 数组别名语法使用不正确，Policy 评估数组属性时有特殊语法要求
solution: 参考文档 [Author policies for array properties on Azure resources] 检查并修正数组别名语法，特别注意 count 表达式和 where 子句的正确写法
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureDev/Dev_ARM:/[ARM] Azure Policy/Support Topics/Policy behavior not as expected/Compliance state and details not as expected

symptom: 资源未被 Policy 扫描评估，或有豁免的资源仍被标记为非合规
rootCause: 资源不在赋值 scope 内、位于 notScopes 排除列表中，或存在针对该资源或其父 scope 的豁免 (exemption)
solution: 1. 验证资源是否在赋值 scope 覆盖范围内；2. 检查赋值的 notScopes 是否包含该资源路径；3. 检查是否存在适用的 exemption
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureDev/Dev_ARM:/[ARM] Azure Policy/Support Topics/Policy behavior not as expected/Compliance state and details not as expected

symptom: Deny/Audit 策略在资源创建/更新时未触发（greenfield），操作正常通过但预期应被阻止或记录
rootCause: 策略赋值的 enforcementMode 未设置为 default，导致 Policy 以审计模式运行不阻止操作
solution: 检查策略赋值的 enforcementMode 属性，将其设置为 Default（区分大小写）；确认不是 DoNotEnforce 模式
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureDev/Dev_ARM:/[ARM] Azure Policy/Support Topics/Policy behavior not as expected/Policy enforcement not as expected

symptom: 策略在 greenfield 评估时未应用到某资源（无活动日志、未阻止、未触发 DINE 部署）
rootCause: 资源不在赋值 scope 内、在 notScopes 中被排除、存在适用的豁免，或策略定义的 type/name/kind 条件中有拼写错误
solution: 1. 检查 EventServiceEntries（通过 correlationId）确认 Policy 是否参与评估；2. 验证资源在赋值 scope 内且不在 notScopes 中；3. 确认无适用的豁免；4. 仔细检查策略定义中 type、name、kind 条件的拼写
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureDev/Dev_ARM:/[ARM] Azure Policy/Support Topics/Policy behavior not as expected/Policy enforcement not as expected

symptom: DINE 策略合规结果不正确：if section 和 then.details 的 type 相同但 name 未指定，同类型的其他资源也能满足 existenceCondition
rootCause: 当 if.field.type 和 then.details.type 相同时，必须指定 then.details.name；否则资源组内任何同类型资源都会满足 DINE 的存在性检查，导致错误合规
solution: 对 DINE 策略中 if.type == then.details.type 的情况，添加 then.details.name 属性并设置为 "[field('fullname')]"
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureDev/Dev_ARM:/[ARM] Azure Policy/Troubleshooting Guides/AINE and DINE checklist

symptom: AINE/DINE 策略对 diagnosticSettings、roleAssignment 等订阅级资源始终评估为非合规
rootCause: existenceScope 默认值为 resourceGroup，导致 Policy 只在资源组层级查找资源；订阅级资源需要在订阅层级查找
solution: 在 then.details 中添加 existenceScope: "Subscription"
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureDev/Dev_ARM:/[ARM] Azure Policy/Troubleshooting Guides/AINE and DINE checklist

symptom: AINE/DINE 策略评估极慢或出现超时（timeout）
rootCause: 策略在 existenceCondition 中包含了 name 或 type 条件，导致 Policy 做 collection GET 获取所有同类资源再逐一评估，而非使用精确 GET 请求
solution: 将 name 和 type 条件从 existenceCondition 移到 then.details.name 和 then.details.type 字段，这样 Policy 引擎会使用精确 GET 而非 collection GET
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureDev/Dev_ARM:/[ARM] Azure Policy/Troubleshooting Guides/AINE and DINE checklist

symptom: DINE 策略触发订阅级部署时失败
rootCause: deploymentScope 默认为 resourceGroup，订阅级模板需要显式设置为 subscription；且订阅级部署必须在 then.details.deployment 中包含 location 属性
solution: 1. 设置 then.details.deploymentScope: "subscription"；2. 在 then.details.deployment 中添加 location 属性；3. 验证 ARM 模板 schema 和结构是否为订阅级部署格式
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureDev/Dev_ARM:/[ARM] Azure Policy/Troubleshooting Guides/AINE and DINE checklist

symptom: 策略对某资源评估结果不正确，原因是该属性在 PUT/PATCH 请求的 payload 中不存在（使用默认值）
rootCause: 资源提供程序不要求 payload 包含所有属性，缺失属性会使用默认值创建资源；策略未考虑属性缺失的情况
solution: 使用 exists 或 notExists 条件显式处理属性不存在的情况，参考 Azure REST API Reference 确认属性默认值，决定缺失时应合规还是非合规
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureDev/Dev_ARM:/[ARM] Azure Policy/Troubleshooting Guides/Condition checklist

symptom: 策略条件永远不匹配，评估结果持续错误，检查逻辑似乎正确但 Policy 不生效
rootCause: Policy 条件中存在类型不匹配：如 Boolean true 与 String "true" 相比，或 Integer 20 与 String "20" 相比；Policy 引擎不会跨类型比较
solution: 确认策略 value 字段使用正确类型：布尔值不加引号（true/false），整数不加引号（20），字符串加引号（"true"、"20"）；对 append 效果，区分 Array 与 Object
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureDev/Dev_ARM:/[ARM] Azure Policy/Troubleshooting Guides/Condition checklist

symptom: 策略使用 split() 等函数时出现评估错误或异常（exception），导致该资源评估失败
rootCause: 策略表达式中的函数未处理边界情况，如 split(field('name'),'-')[1] 在 name 不含 '-' 时，split 结果数组只有 1 项，访问索引 1 返回 null，进而触发异常
solution: 使用 if + greaterOrEquals + length 做边界检查，示例："[if(greaterOrEquals(length(split(field('name'),'-')),2),split(field('name'),'-')[1],'Invalid value')]"
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureDev/Dev_ARM:/[ARM] Azure Policy/Troubleshooting Guides/Condition checklist

symptom: 赋值策略时遇到各类问题：UI 数据不正确、赋值报错、找不到正确的赋值配置

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureDev/Dev_ARM:/[ARM] Azure Policy/Troubleshooting Guides/Confirm if an alias can be made modifiable

symptom: 使用 ARM Complete（完整）模式部署时，资源组中未在模板中定义的现有资源被意外删除
rootCause: Complete 模式会删除目标资源组中所有不在当前模板定义内的现有资源，这是其预期行为；用户在切换到 Complete 模式时未预期此副作用
solution: 在执行 Complete 模式部署前，先运行 what-if 操作预览将被创建/删除/修改的资源（CLI: az deployment group what-if），确认无误后再执行部署。参考 https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-resource-manager/templates/deployment-modes
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureDev/Dev_ARM:/[ARM] Azure Resource Manager (ARM)/Architecture/ARM Templates, Bicep & Deployments/Deployment modes

symptom: Azure CLI 命令失败，错误消息为 'dh key too small'；通常在升级到 Azure CLI 2.37.0+ 后、且客户网络中存在解密 HTTPS 流量的代理时出现
rootCause: Azure CLI 2.37.0 升级到 Python 3.10，Python 3.10 将 OpenSSL SECLEVEL 提升至 2，SECLEVEL 2 要求证书公钥至少 2048 位；客户代理证书仅 1024 位，导致 Python OpenSSL 库拒绝 TLS 握手
solution: 1.（根本解决）升级代理证书使其满足 SECLEVEL 2 要求（公钥 >= 2048 位）；2.（临时方案）回退到旧版 Azure CLI（含旧版 Python，SECLEVEL < 2）并手动安装
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureDev/Dev_ARM:/[ARM] Azure Resource Manager (ARM)/Architecture/Azure CLI/TLS handshake

symptom: Customer cannot create Private Link Association (PLA) at subscription or resource group scope; attempt to configure ARM private link at non-root management group scope results in failure
rootCause: By design, the only allowed scope for the Private Link Association (PLA) resource is the root management group. This enforces that private connectivity is configured for the entire tenant.
solution: Create the PLA at root management group scope. The user needs Owner or Contributor access at root MG level. Prerequisites: Owner on the subscription (for RMPL creation) + Owner/Contributor at root MG + Global Administrator AAD role with elevated access.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureDev/Dev_ARM:/[ARM] Azure Resource Manager (ARM)/Architecture/Private Link for ARM

symptom: Customer tries to set PublicNetworkAccess to Disabled in the Private Link Association (PLA) for ARM but the option is unavailable or not functioning
rootCause: The Public Network Access option in the PLA resource has not been released yet. The property is defined in the schema but the feature has not been enabled.
solution: Feature is not yet available. Customer should monitor for release announcements. Currently there is no workaround to disable public network access via PLA.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureDev/Dev_ARM:/[ARM] Azure Resource Manager (ARM)/Architecture/Private Link for ARM

symptom: Customer attempts to move Azure resources from one Azure Active Directory tenant (directory) to another and the operation fails or is not supported
rootCause: Resources cannot be moved across directories (tenants) in ARM. Cross-directory resource move is not a supported ARM operation.
solution: Resources cannot be moved directly across tenants. As a workaround, a subscription's directory (tenant) can be changed — this scenario is supported by the Azure Subscription Management team. See [Scenarios] Move resources across tenants wiki for additional details.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureDev/Dev_ARM:/[ARM] Azure Resource Manager (ARM)/Architecture/Resource moves

symptom: Customer requests help moving Azure resources from one Azure region to another and the case is routed to ARM team
rootCause: Moving resources across regions is handled by the Azure Resource Mover team, not the ARM team. ARM handles RG-to-RG and subscription-to-subscription moves only.
solution: Route or escalate the case to the Azure Resource Mover team for cross-region move support. ARM team does not own this scenario.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureDev/Dev_ARM:/[ARM] Azure Resource Manager (ARM)/Architecture/Resource moves

symptom: Customer wants to create deny assignments to prevent specific users from inheriting permissions or to exclude certain actions from a broader role assignment, but cannot do so
rootCause: Deny assignments in Azure are created and managed exclusively by Azure services (Azure Blueprints, Azure Managed Apps). End users and administrators cannot directly create their own deny assignments. The feature is reserved for system-managed resources.
solution: There is currently no supported solution for customers to directly exclude permissions or break role assignment inheritance via custom deny assignments. Suggest reviewing the scope of existing role assignments or leveraging Azure Blueprints / Managed Apps patterns if applicable.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureDev/Dev_ARM:/[ARM] Azure Resource Manager (ARM)/Architecture/Role-Based Access Control/Introduction to RBAC

symptom: Customer assigns a role to a user but the user still receives 403 Access Denied errors for several minutes after the assignment was created
rootCause: ARM caches user permissions for up to 30 minutes (from when the cache was initially created on a specific ARM instance). New role assignments are not immediately reflected in the existing cache. The 30-minute window starts from cache creation, not from the role assignment creation time.
solution: Customer can either: (1) wait up to 30 minutes for the cache to expire naturally, or (2) log out and log back in to force a token renewal, which triggers a fresh permissions cache on the next request. Note: hitting a different ARM instance will also result in a fresh cache.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureDev/Dev_ARM:/[ARM] Azure Resource Manager (ARM)/Architecture/Role-Based Access Control/RBAC evaluation on ARM

symptom: User has Owner or Contributor role on a VM (or primary resource) but receives 403 when performing an operation that references a secondary resource (e.g., attaching a NIC to a VM)
rootCause: ARM performs Linked Access Checks on certain operations as declared in the RP manifest's linkedAccessChecks configuration. Even with full permissions on the primary resource, the user must also have the specific linked action permission on the referenced secondary resource (e.g., Microsoft.Network/networkInterfaces/join/action on the NIC).
solution: Identify the required linked action by checking the RP manifest or the error details. Assign the appropriate role granting the linked action to the user on the linked resource. Example: to attach a NIC to a VM, the user needs Microsoft.Network/networkInterfaces/join/action on the NIC resource.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureDev/Dev_ARM:/[ARM] Azure Resource Manager (ARM)/Architecture/Role-Based Access Control/RBAC evaluation on ARM

symptom: Azure portal Tags blade shows empty or incomplete tag list; or customer reports hitting tag name/value combination limit (80k) even though active resources appear to have fewer tags
rootCause: Orphan tags — when resources are deleted, ARM may not clean up the tag name/value combinations from its cache. These orphan entries still count toward the 80k tags summarization limit. If the limit is exceeded, the summarization fails and the tags list appears empty.
solution: Remove orphaned tag names no longer in use using: (1) PowerShell: Remove-AzTag cmdlet, (2) CLI: az tag command, or (3) REST API: Tags Delete Value API. After cleanup, the summarization should resume correctly.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureDev/Dev_ARM:/[ARM] Azure Resource Manager (ARM)/Architecture/Tags

symptom: Tag name or value casing is not preserved as the customer defined it; cost reports or queries show different casing than expected
rootCause: Tag names are case-insensitive for ARM operations (a tag name is retrieved regardless of casing). However, the resource provider may store the tag name with different casing. Tag values are case-sensitive. The resource provider is the source of truth for tag storage, not ARM.
solution: Investigate the specific resource provider storing the tag. If the RP is not preserving the expected casing, the issue should be escalated to the RP team rather than ARM. Confirm whether the issue is with tag names (RP-stored, may vary by RP) or tag values (case-sensitive, must match exactly).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureDev/Dev_ARM:/[ARM] Azure Resource Manager (ARM)/Architecture/Tags

symptom: Customer receives an error when attempting to apply tags to a specific resource type, or tags cannot be added to the resource
rootCause: Only tracked resources (routing type = 'default') support tags. Proxy resources (routing type = 'proxyOnly') and extension resources are not cached by ARM and do not support tags. This is determined by the routing type declared in the resource provider manifest.
solution: Confirm the resource's routing type via the RP manifest (Get RP Manifest TSG). If it is a proxyOnly resource, tags are not supported — this is by design. Inform the customer that tagging is not available for this resource type.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureDev/Dev_ARM:/[ARM] Azure Resource Manager (ARM)/Architecture/Tags

symptom: Terraform AzureRM provider does not support a new Azure resource type, resource property, or API version; terraform plan/apply fails with unsupported resource or missing attribute
rootCause: The AzureRM provider is hardcoded to specific supported resources, properties, and API versions at each release. Any new Azure resource, property, or API version requires a new AzureRM provider version to be released by Hashicorp/Microsoft before it becomes available.
solution: Use the AzAPI Terraform provider as an alternative. AzAPI acts as a thin layer directly over ARM REST APIs and supports all resource types, properties, and API versions (including private preview). Recommended workflow: use AzAPI while the feature is in preview, then migrate to AzureRM once officially released using the AzAPI2AzureRM migration tool.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureDev/Dev_ARM:/[ARM] Azure Resource Manager (ARM)/Architecture/Terraform/Terraform on Azure
